pi_items.verified_value: ["Flesch Reading Ease Score", "Word Count"] (only Flesch column is checked by verifier)
pi_items.source: Official Screaming Frog documentation (https://www.screamingfrog.co.uk/seo-spider/user-guide/tabs/) lists exact column as 'Flesch Reading Ease Score'. Verifier in export_result.sh (lines 70-74) only checks for 'flesch' or 'readability' substring, NOT the exact column names. Verifier.py does NOT check for 'Word Count' column existence at all.
Verifier.py line 74 only checks 'if row_count > 0' and awards points for any non-zero row count. No minimum of 10 rows is enforced anywhere in verifier.py or export_result.sh.

privileged_info_summary: The task crawls https://books.toscrape.com/, a real, publicly accessible sandbox website with 1,000 books designed for web scraping practice. The expected output paths /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports/readability_audit.csv and /home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/hardest_to_read.txt are correctly configured in the setup scripts. However, there is a critical discrepancy: the metadata claims required columns are ["Flesch Reading Ease", "Word Count"], but Screaming Frog's actual column name is "Flesch Reading Ease Score" (not "Flesch Reading Ease"), and the verifier does not check for "Word Count" at all—it only looks for 'flesch' or 'readability' substrings in headers. Additionally, the metadata specifies min_rows: 10, but the verifier imposes no minimum row count threshold, only requiring row_count > 0.
